name: single-site-catalytic-context
prompt: I'm working on TEM-1 beta-lactamase (PDB: 1BTL). I want to mutate His257 to Ala to test catalytic role. Predict stability impact with at least two methods and summarize risks.
expectations: Retrieves or validates an appropriate structure source
Checks structural context around the target residue
Uses at least two complementary stability methods
Reports stability metrics with sign convention clarity
Provides a consensus-style ranking or confidence statement
Flags catalytic-function risk and recommends wet-lab validation

name: thermostability-site-prioritization
prompt: Design stabilizing mutations for lysozyme. Prioritize surface and loop candidates, avoid active-site disruption, and propose a practical screening plan.
expectations: Defines mutation-space constraints before proposing variants
Uses structure-aware rationale for each suggested site
Explains combinatorial limits to avoid search explosion
Returns ranked candidates with evidence provenance
Includes explicit assumptions and functional risk flags

name: sequence-only-track
prompt: I only have FASTA for my enzyme. Design a stability-oriented mutation workflow using AlphaFold-assisted steps and explain what cannot be inferred directly from pLDDT.
expectations: Chooses the sequence/AI-assisted track when structure is missing
States that pLDDT is not a direct stability metric
Describes downstream DDG or learned predictors for ranking
Provides a reproducible batch-oriented workflow

name: numbering-mismatch-guardrail
prompt: My FASTA numbering does not match the PDB chain numbering. I need to run FoldX on A123V, L200I from sequence coordinates. Give a safe mapping-first workflow.
expectations: Detects numbering mismatch risk and calls for explicit mapping
Uses mapping outputs before structure-based scoring
Prevents direct scoring on potentially mis-mapped positions
Documents chain/numbering assumptions in the report

name: consensus-ranking-directionality
prompt: Combine FoldX DDG, Rosetta DDG, and a stability probability score into one ranked table. Ensure metric directionality is handled correctly.
expectations: Aggregates metrics with explicit normalization
Handles mixed metric directions correctly
Reports support count or support fraction across methods
Outputs a final ranked candidate list suitable for triage

name: saturation-library-at-single-site
prompt: Create a saturation mutagenesis plan for site 152 of a protease and prioritize the 19 alternatives by predicted stability for first-round testing.
expectations: Represents all 19 non-WT substitutions at one site
Uses a fast first-pass scoring strategy with caveats
Returns a prioritized shortlist and uncertainty notes
Recommends orthogonal re-scoring or validation for top hits

name: interface-engineering-guardrail
prompt: I want to stabilize a homodimer enzyme by mutating residues near the interface. Provide a safe computational workflow and ranking strategy.
expectations: Locks biological assembly assumptions and interface context
Uses an interface-focused predictor in addition to monomer stability tools
Includes interface-aware scoring and functional risk flags
Provides orthogonal re-scoring or validation for top interface variants

name: stabilizing-bias-awareness
prompt: My model predicts many strongly stabilizing mutations. Give a triage plan that avoids overconfidence and explains known benchmark bias.
expectations: Explicitly describes destabilizing-class bias and uncertainty for stabilizing calls
Treats stabilizing predictions as tentative until orthogonally checked
Uses support fraction or confidence framing in the final shortlist

name: thermostability-objective-specific-ranking
prompt: I only care about thermostability at 60 C. Build an objective-specific ranking workflow and state how you will calibrate score thresholds.
expectations: Uses objective-specific tool choice for thermostability rather than generic stability ranking
Includes a temperature-oriented signal in addition to DDG-style evidence
Defines a calibration panel with known stabilizing/neutral/destabilizing references

name: candidate-site-discovery-before-library
prompt: Before proposing mutations, find and rank candidate mutation sites for my enzyme from FASTA+PDB, avoid catalytic/cofactor/disulfide residues, and output site specs for library generation.
expectations: Performs candidate-site discovery before mutation-library enumeration
Uses structural context including active-shell distance and exposure or flexibility
Applies blocklist guardrails for catalytic or cofactor or disulfide positions
Outputs ranked site candidates with rationale and site specs for downstream generation
